title: LM Studio server
description: Connect to a local LM Studio native v1 server.

fields.label: Endpoint URL
fields.description: Base URL of LM Studio's server. The plugin appends /api/v1/chat and /api/v1/models; a trailing slash is normalized. Non-default hosts must also be added to permissions.network.allow.

fields.label: Model fallback
fields.placeholder: leave blank to use the loaded model
fields.description: Fallback model id. A spawn-time model overrides this; matching preset model bindings select prompts and params.

fields.label: Max tool rounds
fields.description: Maximum tool calls per user turn before the shell stops the loop. Range: 1-64. Higher values are useful for watcher/probe presets.

title: Presets
description: Open-shell presets select the system prompt, model binding, and native LM Studio parameters for new sessions.

fields.label: Default preset
fields.description: Preset id to use when the chosen model has no bound preset and the session does not specify ctx.preset.

fields.title: Preset objects
fields.body: Edit config.yaml for the presets array. Shape: presets: [{ id, name, systemPrompt?, model?, params? }]. A preset with model binds to that exact model id and wins at session init; if it has no systemPrompt, the default preset prompt is used. Preset params may include LM Studio request fields such as response_format plus plugin controls such as maxToolRounds.

title: Structured output
description: Optional native response_format payload passed through to LM Studio /api/v1/chat.

fields.title: responseFormat
fields.body: Set responseFormat in config.yaml to pass a structured-output response_format object to LM Studio. Example: responseFormat: { type: json_schema, json_schema: { name: watcher_report, schema: { type: object, properties: { state: { type: string } }, required: [state] } } }
